title: Count distinct field values in JSONL via `jq -r '.field' | sort -u | wc -l`
trigger: When summarizing a JSONL field's distinct values and `jq` is available
summary: `jq -r '.<field>' <path>` extracts one value per line. Pipe through `sort -u` for deduplication and `wc -l` for the count. If `jq` is missing: `python3 -c "import json; print(len({json.loads(l)['<field>'] for l in open('<path>')}))"`.

title: Count log lines matching a token via `grep -c '<token>' <path>`
trigger: When counting occurrences of a literal token in a text file
summary: `grep -c '<token>' <path>` returns just the count, no lines. Use `-i` for case-insensitive, `-E` for regex, `-w` for whole-word. To count lines matching ANY of several tokens: `grep -cE 'ERROR|FATAL|CRITICAL'`. Avoid `grep '<token>' | wc -l

title: Inspect gzip contents via `gunzip -c <path> | head`
trigger: When you need a peek at a gzipped file's content without fully decompressing
summary: `gunzip -c <path>` writes decompressed output to stdout (the `-c` keeps the original file). Pipe through `head -n N` for the first N lines. Python-only path: `gzip.open(path, 'rt').readline()`.

title: List TAR entries via `tar -tvf <path>`
trigger: When you need TAR entry names + metadata and a unix `tar` is available
summary: `tar -tvf <path>` lists entries one per line with mode, owner, size, mtime, name — strictly richer than `tarfile.getnames()`. Python fallback: `tarfile.open(path).getnames()` (names only) or `.getmembers()` (full metadata).

title: List ZIP entries via stdlib `zipfile.ZipFile().namelist()`
trigger: When you need ZIP entry names and Python is available
summary: Use `zipfile.ZipFile(path).namelist()` — one call returns a list of strings. The stdlib reads the central directory; no struct manipulation needed. Use `infolist()` for sizes/dates/CRC32s alongside names.

title: Read BMP width and bit depth via the BITMAPINFOHEADER offsets
trigger: When you need BMP dimensions or bit depth from raw bytes
summary: Validate the first 2 bytes are `BM` (the file header). The BITMAPINFOHEADER begins at byte 14. Width is at file offset 18 (uint32 LE, 4 bytes); bit depth (`biBitCount`) is at offset 28 (uint16 LE). `struct.unpack('<I', data[18:22])` and `st

title: Read GIF version and dimensions from the first 10 bytes via stdlib struct
trigger: When you need GIF version + dimensions without Pillow
summary: GIF header layout: bytes 0-5 are the signature ASCII (`GIF87a` or `GIF89a`). Bytes 6-7 are width (uint16 little-endian); bytes 8-9 are height (uint16 little-endian). Read 10 bytes; decode signature as ASCII and `struct.unpack('<HH', data[6:

title: Read PNG width and height from the IHDR chunk via stdlib struct
trigger: When you need PNG dimensions and Pillow / image tools may be unavailable
summary: Validate the 8-byte signature `\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n` first. The IHDR chunk follows immediately (4-byte length, 4-byte type 'IHDR'). Width and height are the first 8 bytes of the IHDR data — at file offsets 16 and 20, each a big-endian 4-byte u

title: Read WAV sample rate / channels / bit depth via stdlib `wave`
trigger: When you need WAV header fields and Python is available
summary: `wave.open(path, 'rb')` returns a reader with `.getframerate()`, `.getnchannels()`, `.getsampwidth()`, `.getnframes()`. Stdlib parses the RIFF container and `fmt ` subchunk. Use a `with` statement. Note: `wave` only handles uncompressed PCM

title: Read WebP dimensions by dispatching on the RIFF subchunk type
trigger: When you need WebP dimensions and the Pillow webp plugin may be missing
summary: WebP is a RIFF container. Validate bytes 0-3 = `RIFF` and 8-11 = `WEBP`. Read the 4-byte chunk type at offset 12 to dispatch: `VP8 ` (lossy — width/height at offset 26-29 as 14-bit LE pairs), `VP8L` (lossless — 14-bit (width-1) / (height-1)

title: Skip _index.jsonl when AGENTS.md scope warning rules out the task
trigger: When the task is trivially answerable without external knowledge AND AGENTS.md's scope warning explicitly says don't read the wiki for trivial tasks
summary: AGENTS.md ships with: 'Don't read me for trivial tasks (typo fix, single-line refactor) or topics clearly outside the wiki's scope.' If the task is a single deterministic conversion, computation, or lookup that requires no external context,

title: Skip the parser for tiny INI files — Read the file directly
trigger: When the INI file is small (<50 lines) and you need one specific key
summary: Just Read the file. INI's syntax is human-readable; a one-shot value lookup doesn't need `configparser`. For larger files, or when you need iteration / case-insensitive sections / interpolation, switch to `configparser.ConfigParser()`.

title: Use stdlib `csv.reader` with `newline=''` for CSVs that may have quoted commas
trigger: When parsing CSV and any field might contain a comma, newline, or embedded quote
summary: Open with `newline=''` (REQUIRED — without it, embedded newlines inside quoted fields break the row boundary). Then `csv.reader(f)` walks rows respecting RFC 4180 quoting. Naive `line.split(',')` is wrong whenever a field contains `","`.

title: Validate wiki applicability via _index.jsonl before forcing a citation
trigger: When AGENTS.md tells you to consult the wiki, but the user's task may be outside its scope
summary: After reading AGENTS.md, read `_index.jsonl` end-to-end and check whether any row's tags or trigger text overlaps your task's topical tags. If nothing overlaps, the wiki has nothing to offer — proceed with the task using your own approach.

title: Walk the Exif sub-IFD via tag 0x8769 to read camera-optics fields
trigger: When extracting LensModel / FocalLength / aperture / ISO from a JPEG and Pillow / piexif / exiftool may be missing
summary: JPEG EXIF lives behind APP1 marker `0xFFE1`. Inside, after the literal `Exif\x00\x00`, sits the TIFF block. Walk IFD0 to find tag `0x8769` whose value is the offset of the Exif sub-IFD. Re-enter the IFD parser at that offset. Camera-optics
